---
title: "Data Preparation"
---

## Dataset Overview
The dataset used for this analysis is drawn from the VAST 2025 Mini Challenge 1 (MC1), a synthetic music industry knowledge graph modelling relationship among songs, albums, musical groups, and record labels. It was sourced from two origins: a crowdsourced repository of musical influence, and a journalist-compiled record of chart performance.


## Graph Schema

### Node Variables

| Variables | Type | Applicable Node Types | Description |
|---------|--------|---------|--------|
|Node Type |String |All |Categorical type of the node|
|name|String|All|Full name of the person, group, label, song, or album
|stage_name|String|Person|Artist/stage name if different from legal name (near-complete nulls, ~97–99%)
|genre|String|Song, Album|Musical genre of the work|
|release_date|Integer (year)|Song, Album|Year the work was released|
|written_date|Integer (year)|Song, Album|Year the work was written|
|notable|Boolean|Song, Album|Whether the work appeared on a top record chart|
|notoriety_date|Integer (year)|Song, Album|Year the work first appeared on a top chart (only present if notable = True)|
|single|Boolean|Song|Whether the song was released as a standalone single rather than as part of an album|

### Edge Variables

|Edge Type|Source|Target|Description|
|---------|--------|---------|--------|
|PerformerOf|Person / MusicalGroup|Song / Album|Source node performed the target work|
|ComposerOf|Person|Song / Album|Source node composed the target work|
|LyricistOf|Person|Song / Album|Source node wrote lyrics for the target work|
|ProducerOf|Person / RecordLabel|Song / Album / Person / MusicalGroup|Source node participated in the production of the target|
|RecordedOf|Song / Album|RecordLabel|Target label aided in recording the source work|
|DistributedBy|Song / Album|RecordLabel|Target label aided in distributing the source work|
|MemberOf|Person|MusicalGroup|Source node is / was a member of the target group|
|InStyleOf|Song / Album|Song / Album / Person / MusicalGroup|Source node was performed at least partly in the style of the target|
|InterpolatesFrom|Song / Album|Song / Album|Source node interpolated a melody from the target|
|CoverOf|Song / Album|Song / Album|Source node is a cover version of the target|
|LyricalReferenceTo|Song / Album|Song / Album |Source node makes a lyrical reference to the target|
DirectlySamples|Song / Album|Song / Album|Source node directly reuses an audio recording from the target via sampling|

## Cleaning and Preparation Pipeline

The raw graph was exported as separate CSVs for nodes (mc1_nodes.csv) and edges (mc1_edges.csv) and processed through the following pipeline, producing a final enriched edge table edges_prepped.csv with 37,857 rows and 23 columns. <br><br>
**Step 1: Data Loading** <br><br>
The node list, edge list, and raw JSON graph were loaded and inspected for structure and field coverage.<br><br>
**Step 2: Node–Edge Join** <br><br>
The raw edge list contained only source and target node IDs with no contextual information. Node attributes were joined onto the edge table twice via pandas left merge — once for the source endpoint and once for the target — producing a flat, denormalised table where each row carries full metadata about both connected entities.<br><br>
**Step 3: Type Casting** <br><br>
Date columns were coerced from mixed-type floats to numeric. Boolean fields (notable, single) had nulls filled with False, as the absence of a positive label in this context implies the attribute does not apply.<br><br>
**Step 4: Year Derivation** <br><br>
Unified source_year and target_year columns were created using release_date as the primary source, falling back to written_date when release information was absent. Both are stored as nullable Int64 to correctly preserve missing values as NaN rather than converting them to zero.<br><br>
**Step 5: Integer Conversion** <br><br>
Remaining float year values (e.g. 2026.0) were cleaned to proper integers using pandas Int64 dtype across all date columns.<br><br>
**Step 6: Column Pruning** <br><br>
Four columns were dropped: stage_name (source and target) due to near-complete nulls, and written_date (source and target) as their values were already subsumed by the derived year columns.<br><br>
**Step 7: Column Reordering** <br><br>
The final 23 columns were reordered for readability: edge metadata first, then source node attributes, then target node attributes.
